name: Esther Eskelson's Danish Raspberry Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Milk chocolate or white chocolate chips
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the chocolate in the top of a double boiler over hot (not boiling) water.
2. Remove from heat and cool. Cream butter and sugar in a large bowl until light.
3. Add egg and melted chocolate; beat until fluffy. Stir in cake flour, vanilla, and salt until well blended. Cover and refrigerate until firm, about 1 hour.
4. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Lightly grease cookie sheets or line with parchment paper.
5. Divide dough into 4 equal parts and divide each part into two pieces. Roll each piece into a rope about 12 inches long on a lightly floured board. The ropes should be about the thickness of a finger.
6. Place the cookies 2 inches apart on the prepared cookie sheets. With your finger tip, make an indentation along the length of each rope.
7. Bake for 8 minutes or until firm.
8. Meanwhile, melt chocolate chips in a small bowl over hot water. If using both kinds of chocolate, melt separately. Stir until smooth.
9. Stir preserves and spoon into a pastry bag fitted with a 1/4 inch tip. You can use a plastic bag with a corner snipped off for scissors.
10. When removing cookies from the oven, press preserves down the indentation in each cookie strip. Return to the oven for 2 minutes and remove to wire racks. While cookies are still warm, drizzle melted chocolate over the tops and cut into 1 inch diagonal pieces. Refrigerate until the chocolate has set.
